name: ACE Cash Express
description_short: At 3200 S Lancaster Rd Ste 170B, Dallas, TX 75216, ACE Cash Express can be reached by calling 1-214-375-6209.
description_long:
ACE Cash Express is an established financial services company operating physical store locations across the United States, with a significant presence in multiple states including Colorado. The company has been serving consumers seeking fast access to emergency funds and other financial services for an extended period. ACE Cash Express provides a variety of short-term lending and cash access products designed to meet immediate financial needs. Their service model combines in-store locations with online options to reach customers through multiple channels.

The company's primary service offerings include installment loans, title loans, check cashing, bill payment services, money transfers, and tax services. They also offer cards and business services through their locations. The website indicates they provide online loan options in addition to their physical store network. Their emergency lending products are positioned as fast-access solutions, with the company emphasizing quick funding capabilities to address urgent financial situations.

ACE Cash Express distinguishes itself through its multi-service approach, combining traditional payday and title lending with additional financial services like check cashing, money transfers, and tax preparation. This bundled service model allows customers to address multiple financial needs at single locations. The company maintains a widespread network of physical stores, which provides accessibility for customers who prefer in-person transactions and immediate service. Their online lending platform extends reach beyond their physical footprint.

As an emergency cash lender, ACE Cash Express operates in a sector with significant regulatory variation by state and notable consumer finance considerations. The company's focus on emergency short-term lending means products come with higher costs typical of this category. The 404 errors on the website suggest outdated or broken location pages, which may reflect operational challenges or website maintenance issues. Consumers should carefully review specific loan terms, APR disclosures, and state-specific regulations before borrowing, as emergency loans carry substantial costs compared to traditional lending.

company_info.headquarters: 3200 S Lancaster Rd Ste 170B, Dallas, TX 75216

pros: Multiple service options beyond loans (check cashing, money transfers, tax services, bill payments)
Physical store locations available for same-day in-person service
Online loan application options available for remote access
Established company with nationwide presence across multiple states
Title loan options for customers with vehicle collateral
Business services available in addition to consumer products
Installment loan options that may have better terms than payday loans
cons: Website has broken 404 pages for Denver location, suggesting outdated or incomplete information
As an emergency lender, products carry significantly higher APR costs than traditional loans
State-specific restrictions mean some loan types unavailable in certain states like Colorado (based on website message)
Limited transparency on specific APR rates and fees visible on provided website content
Hurricane Ida closure message suggests website content is outdated (Hurricane Ida was 2021)
best_for: Consumers needing same-day cash access who prefer in-person transactions
Individuals with vehicle collateral seeking title loan options
Customers requiring multiple financial services bundled at one location
diagnosis: ACE Cash Express is best for consumers with immediate funding needs who have access to a physical location and prefer in-person transactions or who have vehicle collateral for a title loan. The primary caveat is that emergency loans carry substantially higher costs (APR) than traditional lending, so borrowers should only use these products when truly urgent and should explore alternatives like credit union PALs or employer advances first.
